comment_text: When questioned H Bowman stated that he was instructed to ride the horse in a conservative manner in the early stages to ensure that it ran out a strong 2000 Metres. He added whilst he had expected to settle back in the field he had not anticipated the race being run inside of standard time in the early and middle stages. He said near the 500 Metres he commenced to shift to the outside of WINNING WING, however, when that runner shifted out at the same point he then elected to save ground and improve to the inside of WINNING WING near the 450 Metres whereby MY WISH responded well to his riding. He said after making good ground between the 400 Metres and the 150 Metres the horse’s run ended which he attributed to the early and middle stages of the race being run inside of standard time. Trainer M Newnham confirmed the instructions and stated he issued those instructions to allow the horse to run out a strong 2000 Metres.

comment_text: When questioned B Avdulla stated that from barrier 2 and when the connections were of the opinion there was not strong pace in the event he was given the option to lead if BEAUTY JOY jumped well. He said as a result when his mount commenced well he elected to allow the horse to stride forward and lead the event. He added that BEAUTY JOY raced keenly throughout the early and middle stages and as a result ran inside of standard time despite his efforts to set a more sustainable pace. He said due to the manner the horse raced throughout the early and middle stages it did not respond to his riding in the Home Straight. Trainer A S Cruz confirmed that he had given B Avdulla the option to go forward. He said he issued those instructions as BEAUTY JOY has been finishing its races off only fairly due to racing keenly in the early stages when settling back in the field.

comment_text: Jumped only fairly. The Stewards deferred the declaration of weighed-in as they were of the view that an incident had occurred near the 300 Metres which cast sufficient doubt on whether MASTER TRILLION should be declared the winner. Subsequently J Orman, the rider of the second placegetter, THE GOLDEN KNIGHT, lodged a protest/objection against MASTER TRILLION being declared the winner alleging interference near the 300 Metres. After considering the evidence the Stewards found that THE GOLDEN KNIGHT had established clear running between GOLDEN FORTUNE and MASTER TRILLION approaching the 300 Metres. A short distance later MASTER TRILLION (K C Leung) then shifted out, resulting in THE GOLDEN KNIGHT having to be checked when crowded between GOLDEN FORTUNE and MASTER TRILLION. Having regard to the margin of a head between the two horses at the finish of the race and the manner in which THE GOLDEN KNIGHT finished the race off the Stewards were comfortably satisfied that had the interference not have occurred, THE GOLDEN KNIGHT would have finished in advance of MASTER TRILLION. On that basis the protest/objection was sustained and the placings amended to read No. 4, THE GOLDEN KNIGHT, 1st; No. 3, MASTER TRILLION, 2nd; No. 9, MEGA MASTERMIND, 3rd; and No. 13, FLYING AMANI, 4th. At a subsequent inquiry, K C Leung pleaded guilty to a charge of careless riding [Rule 100(1)] in that near the 300 Metres he allowed his mount to shift out when not clear of THE GOLDEN KNIGHT, resulting in THE GOLDEN KNIGHT having to be checked to avoid MASTER TRILLION. K C Leung was suspended from riding in races for one Hong Kong raceday to be conducted on Wednesday, 18 March 2026 at Happy Valley. In assessing penalty, the Stewards determined the carelessness grade 1 and the consequences grade 2 and also considered his good race riding record. Sent for sampling post-race.

comment_text: Bumped on jumping. When questioned B Avdulla stated that he was given no specific instructions where to settle but not to make use of the horse in the early stages as it has had a tendency to race keenly which had resulted in the sheepskin cheek pieces being removed from the horse for today’s race. He said this resulted in STORMY KNIGHT settling back in the field in the early stages, particularly with the early sectionals being run inside of standard time. He added that throughout the middle stages he was obliged to place some pressure on his mount to hold the position to the inside of POET’S REIGN and when the pace steadied after the 600 Metres he was able to improve his position to be racing behind the winner YEE CHEONG GLORY. He added in the early stages of the Home Straight he was reluctant to place full pressure on his mount as he had observed LIVE WIRE, which was racing in advance of him, having difficulty obtaining clear running and he was concerned if he placed his mount under pressure it would not have clear room to improve into. He said from the 300 Metres when he was satisfied there was an opportunity to improve he then placed STORMY KNIGHT under full pressure but it took some time to respond and from the 100 Metres commenced to make good ground before being held up over the concluding stages. Trainer D A Hayes confirmed the instructions and stated that he issued those instructions as the horse does have a tendency to race keenly in its races.

comment_text: Prior to declaring weighed-in the Stewards gave consideration as to whether SKY PHOENIX had been denied a fair start. After taking evidence from Senior Starter Mr A Speechley it was established that as the start was effected SKY PHOENIX reared simultaneously, resulting in its rider K Teetan being dislodged and SKY PHOENIX completing the course riderless. As the incident occurred simultaneously to the start being effected the horse was declared a runner. Before being allowed to race again, SKY PHOENIX will be required to perform satisfactorily in consecutive barrier trials. K Teetan was subsequently examined by the Chief Medical Officer and passed fit to fulfil his remaining riding engagements.

comment_text: When questioned Z Purton stated that from the outside barrier he was instructed to ride the horse in a conservative manner in the early stages and it was agreed that he was to endeavour to improve to the inside on the Home Turn as he felt it had been difficult to make up ground when making a wide run. He said the instructions to ride the horse in a conservative manner were also due to the fact that the horse had finished the race off only fairly at its last start when ridden in a forward position. He said in accordance with those instructions from the outside barrier he shifted the horse across behind runners where it settled at the tail of the field. He added after following SUPER UNICORN into the race he improved to the inside of that runner in accordance with the pre-race plan which resulted in him improving behind JOLTIN. He said after obtaining clear running to the outside of JOLTIN near the 350 Metres he placed his mount under full pressure but MIGHTY MASTS raced greenly and was inclined to shift ground. He said for this reason he was unable to use the whip on the horse as he was concerned had he done so it would have shifted additional ground and therefore he rode the horse vigorously hands and heels to the finish of the race. Trainer W Y So confirmed the instructions issued and expressed satisfaction with the manner in which Jockey Purton had ridden the horse. Sent for sampling post-race.

comment_text: When questioned regarding his riding in the Home Straight A Atzeni stated that he was instructed to hold his mount up in the early stages of the Home Straight as STAR RISE possesses a short sprint. He said after following the favourite and the winner into the race he was content to remain racing behind that runner having regard to his instructions and also HOT DELIGHT was providing him with cover to continue to improve. He added that after shifting to the outside of HOT DELIGHT approaching the 250 Metres STAR RISE lay in as it was placed under pressure. comment_text: Bumped after travelling a short distance. When questioned J Orman stated that he was asked to go forward but obtain cover if possible. He said in the early stages, after commencing well, he was obliged to allow his mount to continue forward to cross DASHING PAL and SUPER SICARIO which resulted in ANOTHER ZONDA being positioned outside the leader FRANTANCK. He added approaching the 1100 Metres ANOTHER ZONDA, which was racing keenly, improved past FRANTANCK and when its rider did not contest the lead he elected to continue forward to cross FRANTANCK after entering the Back Straight near the 900 Metres. When questioned if there was an opportunity to maintain a position to the outside of FRANTANCK to avoid setting a faster pace J Orman stated that as his mount was racing keenly and he had improved past FRANTANCK he felt that his mount’s best opportunity was to continue forward and cross that runner rather than having to continually restrain his mount. He further stated that after improving to the lead he was able to reduce the pace through the middle stages of the event in an attempt to assist his mount to finish off in the Home Straight but ANOTHER ZONDA failed to respond to his riding. Trainer Y S Tsui confirmed the preference was to obtain cover. A veterinary inspection immediately following the race which included an endoscopic examination showed a substantial amount of blood in the horse’s trachea. Before being allowed to race again, ANOTHER ZONDA will be subjected to an official veterinary examination.

comment_text: Bumped after travelling a short distance. From the outside barrier was shifted across behind runners in the early stages. When questioned regarding his riding in the middle stages and the reason he did not improve to the outside of CAUSEWAY KING sooner than he did H Bowman stated that after settling back in the field, making the turn near the 500 Metres his mount lay out and became unbalanced which resulted in him having to take hold to balance his horse. He added that passing the 450 Metres he expected CAUSEWAY KING would shift out as it was racing in a loose one-off position and as a result he remained racing behind that horse as his preference was to improve to its inside. He said when CAUSEWAY KING did not shift out he then was obliged to improve to that runner’s outside but as he did so CAUSEWAY KING then shifted out near the 350 Metres, resulting in TOGETHER WE VALUE having to make a wide run on the Home Turn. After considering his explanation the Stewards found the tactics adopted by Jockey Bowman were reasonable in the circumstances.

comment_text: Prior to declaring weighed-in Trainer K L Man and Jockey C Y Ho viewed the Stewards’ patrol films to ascertain if there were grounds for a protest/objection against VOLCANIC SPARK (M F Poon) being declared the second placegetter. When connections declined to lodge a protest/objection and the Stewards were satisfied there were not sufficient grounds having regard to the 1 ½ length margin between the two horses at the finish of the race, weighed-in was declared on the numbers semaphored by the Judge. At a subsequent inquiry M F Poon pleaded guilty to a charge of careless riding [Rule 100(1)] in that near the 150 Metres he allowed his mount to shift out whilst riding it along when insufficiently clear of DOUBLE WIN, resulting in DOUBLE WIN being taken from its rightful running and having to be restrained to avoid the heels of VOLCANIC SPARK. M F Poon was suspended from riding in races for one Hong Kong raceday to be conducted on Sunday, 12 April 2026 at Sha Tin. In assessing penalty, the Stewards determined the carelessness grade 2 and the consequences grade 2 and also considered his excellent race riding record.

comment_text: Near the 250 Metres became unbalanced when it made contact with SERANGOON due to both runners shifting ground. When questioned regarding his riding in the early stages and the reason he made a forward move from the 1500 Metres when the early stages of the event were being run inside of standard time A Atzeni stated that he was given the instructions to go forward and as the race appeared to lack pace he was also given the option of leading if circumstances permitted. He said after being crossed by FORTUNE KINGO and GREY CHARGER in the early stages he held a position behind those two runners. He said near the 1500 Metres he felt the pace eased, particularly when R Kingscote, rider of GREY CHARGER, restrained his mount. He said as a consequence this resulted in STORM RUNNER racing keenly at which point he elected to shift out near the 1500 Metres to go forward. He added that as he then continued forward to cross FORTUNE KINGO and GREY CHARGER those two runners held their position which resulted in him having to make more use of his mount along the Back Straight where he was able to cross GREY CHARGER near the 1200 Metres. He said due to the manner in which he was obliged to ride his mount in the middle stages to cross GREY CHARGER, STORM RUNNER was left without a finishing effort in the Home Straight. Trainer P C Ng confirmed the instructions issued. He stated that whilst his preference would have been for STORM RUNNER to maintain its position with cover near the 1500 Metres he accepted Jockey Atzeni’s explanation as to why he elected to shift out and start a forward move. After considering his explanation the Stewards advised Jockey Atzeni that, having regard to the early stages of the event being run inside of standard time, he made an error of judgement by not making more effort to remain racing behind GREY CHARGER to avoid having to make too much use of his mount to cross the early leaders. A veterinary inspection immediately following the race did not show any significant findings. The performance of STORM RUNNER was considered disappointing as compared to its previous race starts. Before being allowed to race again, STORM RUNNER will be required to perform to the satisfaction of the Stewards in a barrier trial and be subjected to an official veterinary examination.
